title: $6.5 Billion Healthcare Fraud Bust — 90 Doctors Charged
script: 455 arrests. One morning! The Justice Department just ran the 2026 National Health Care Fraud Takedown — the program's largest ever single-day operation. 455 defendants charged. 90 of them are licensed doctors and other medical professionals. Total alleged fraud: over $6.5 billion in false claims. The schemes: billing Medicare and Medicaid for treatments patients never received, handing out illegal opioid prescriptions for cash, filing fake diagnoses so insurers pay for patients who aren't even sick. Prosecutors say some of these operations had been running for years. Today was collection day. The bust spans multiple states. If you've ever received a medical bill for something that didn't feel right, this is part of why. How does $6.5 billion in fake healthcare claims go undetected long enough to become the biggest bust of the year?
